subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I: NiCN42- is square planar and diamagnetic complex, with dsp2 hybridization for Ni but NiCO4 is tetrahedral, paramagnetic and with sp3 hybridication for Ni.

Statement II : NiCl42- and NiCO4 both have same d-electron configuration, have same geometry and are paramagnetic.

In light the above statements, choose the correct answer form the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I: NiCN42- is square planar and diamagnetic complex, with dsp2 hybridization for Ni but NiCO4 is tetrahedral, paramagnetic and with sp3 hybridication for Ni.

Statement II : NiCl42- and NiCO4 both have same d-electron configuration, have same geometry and are paramagnetic.

In light the above statements, choose the correct answer form the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
Statement I is correct but statement II is false.
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is true.
Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
solution:
NiCN42-: d8 configuration, SFL, sq. planar splitting dsp2, diamagnetic.

NiCO4 : d10 config (after excitation), SFL, tetrahedral splitting sp3, diamagnetic.

NiCl42-: d8 config, WFL, tetrahedral splitting sp3, paramagnetic(2 unpaired e-).

Electronic configuration of Ni atom in NiCO4

subject: Chemistry
q: The number of isoelectronic species among $\mathrm{Sc}^{3+}, \mathrm{Cr}^{2+}, \mathrm{Mn}^{3+}, \mathrm{Co}^{3+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ is ' $\mathrm{n}^{\prime}$. If ' $\mathrm{n}^{\prime}$ moles of AgCl is formed during the reaction of complex with formula $\mathrm{CoCl}_{3}(\mathrm{en})_{2} \mathrm{NH}_{3}$ with excess of $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ solution, then the number of electrons present in the $t_{2 g}$ orbital of the complex is $\_\_\_\_$.
question: The number of isoelectronic species among $\mathrm{Sc}^{3+}, \mathrm{Cr}^{2+}, \mathrm{Mn}^{3+}, \mathrm{Co}^{3+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ is ' $\mathrm{n}^{\prime}$. If ' $\mathrm{n}^{\prime}$ moles of AgCl is formed during the reaction of complex with formula $\mathrm{CoCl}_{3}(\mathrm{en})_{2} \mathrm{NH}_{3}$ with excess of $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ solution, then the number of electrons present in the $t_{2 g}$ orbital of the complex is $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
For the complex CoCl₃(en)₂NH₃, cobalt is in +3 oxidation state with electron configuration [Ar]3d⁶.
In an octahedral field with strong field ligands (ethylenediamine, ammonia, chloride), Co³⁺ forms a low-spin complex with $t_{2g}^6 e_g^0$ configuration.
The $t_{2g}$ orbitals consist of $d_{xy}$, $d_{xz}$, and $d_{yz}$.
With all 6 electrons filling the three $t_{2g}$ orbitals, the total number of electrons in the $t_{2g}$ orbital set is 6.

subject: Chemistry
q: Total number of relatively more stable isomer(s) possible for octahedral complex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Cu</mi><msub><mfenced><mi>en</mi></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><mi>SCN</mi></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> will be____
question: Total number of relatively more stable isomer(s) possible for octahedral complex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Cu</mi><msub><mfenced><mi>en</mi></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><mi>SCN</mi></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> will be____
solution:
This octahedral complex is of M(AA)2b2 type structure in which AA is bidentate ligand and b is monodentate ligand. This can exist in two geometrical isomeric form cis and trans. Out of which trans form is relatively more stable isomer. Now due to ambidentate ligand SCN- it can also shows linkage isomersim.

Cuen2SCN2

So, relatively more stable trans isomers will have three possible isomers.

Co NH 3 3 Cl 3

subject: Chemistry
q: A solution containing $2.675 \mathrm{~g}$ of $\mathrm{CoCl}_3 .6 \mathrm{NH}_3$ (molar mass $=267.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) is passed through a cation exchanger. The chloride ions obtained in solution were treated with excess of $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ to give $4.78 \mathrm{~g}$ of $\mathrm{AgCl}$ (molar mass $=143.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ). The formula of the complex is (At. Mass of $\mathrm{Ag}=108 \mathrm{u}$ )
question: A solution containing $2.675 \mathrm{~g}$ of $\mathrm{CoCl}_3 .6 \mathrm{NH}_3$ (molar mass $=267.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) is passed through a cation exchanger. The chloride ions obtained in solution were treated with excess of $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ to give $4.78 \mathrm{~g}$ of $\mathrm{AgCl}$ (molar mass $=143.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ). The formula of the complex is (At. Mass of $\mathrm{Ag}=108 \mathrm{u}$ )
solution:
$\mathrm{CoCl}_3 .6 \mathrm{NH}_3 \rightarrow \times \mathrm{Cl}^{-} \stackrel{\mathrm{AgNO}_3}{\longrightarrow} \times \mathrm{AgCl} \downarrow$
$\mathrm{n}(\mathrm{AgCl})=\mathrm{x}\left(\mathrm{CoCl}_3 .6 \mathrm{NH}_3\right)$
$\frac{4.78}{143.5}=\times \frac{2.675}{267.5} \quad \therefore \mathrm{x}=3$
$\therefore$ The complex is $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right] \mathrm{Cl}_3$

q: The spin-only magnetic moment value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ion (in gaseous state) from the pairs <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Cr</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Cr</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Co</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Co</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> that has negative standard electrode potential, is_____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
question: The spin-only magnetic moment value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ion (in gaseous state) from the pairs <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Cr</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Cr</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Mn</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Co</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mi>Co</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> that has negative standard electrode potential, is_____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
solution:
Negative value of electrode potential for M3+M2+ is for Cr+3Cr+2

ECr+3Cr+20=-0.41V

Cr+2 acts as a reducing agent readily

Electronic configuration of Cr+3 ion is

Ar4s03d3

Number of unpaired electrons,n=3

Magnetic moment,

μ=nn+2B.M   =33+2B.M

=15 B.M≃4 B.M

q: The d- electronic configuration of an octahedral Co(II) complex having magnetic moment of 3.95 BM is:
question: The d- electronic configuration of an octahedral Co(II) complex having magnetic moment of 3.95 BM is:
solution:

Hence number of unpaired electron $=3$
$\mathrm{Co}^{2+} \Rightarrow 3 d^7=\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}^5 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}^2$

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: The number of paramagnetic species among $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_{6}\right]^{3-},\left[\mathrm{TiF}_{6}\right]^{3-}$, $\mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}$ and $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]^{3-}$ is 3.
Statement II: $\mathrm{K}_{4}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]<\mathrm{K}_{3}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]<\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{SO}_{4} \cdot \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}<\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{Cl}_{3}$ is the correct order in terms of number of unpaired electron(s) present in the complexes.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: The number of paramagnetic species among $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_{6}\right]^{3-},\left[\mathrm{TiF}_{6}\right]^{3-}$, $\mathrm{V}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{5}$ and $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]^{3-}$ is 3.
Statement II: $\mathrm{K}_{4}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]<\mathrm{K}_{3}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]<\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{SO}_{4} \cdot \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}<\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{Cl}_{3}$ is the correct order in terms of number of unpaired electron(s) present in the complexes.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
solution:
Statement I: Analysis of paramagnetic species.
1. $[CoF_6]^{3-}$: $Co^{3+}$ is $3d^6$. $F^-$ is a weak field ligand (WFL), so configuration is $t_{2g}^4 e_g^2$. Unpaired electrons ($n$) = 4. Paramagnetic.
2. $[TiF_6]^{3-}$: $Ti^{3+}$ is $3d^1$. $n = 1$. Paramagnetic.
3. $V_2O_5$: $V^{5+}$ is $3d^0$. $n = 0$. Diamagnetic.
4. $[Fe(CN)_6]^{3-}$: $Fe^{3+}$ is $3d^5$. $CN^-$ is a strong field ligand (SFL), so configuration is $t_{2g}^5 e_g^0$. $n = 1$. Paramagnetic.
Total paramagnetic species = 3. Statement I is true.
Statement II: Analysis of unpaired electrons.
1. $K_4[Fe(CN)_6]$: $Fe^{2+}$ ($3d^6$) with SFL $\rightarrow t_{2g}^6 e_g^0$, $n = 0$.
2. $K_3[Fe(CN)_6]$: $Fe^{3+}$ ($3d^5$) with SFL $\rightarrow t_{2g}^5 e_g^0$, $n = 1$.
3. $[Fe(H_2O)_6]SO_4 \cdot H_2O$: $Fe^{2+}$ ($3d^6$) with WFL $\rightarrow t_{2g}^4 e_g^2$, $n = 4$.
4. $[Fe(H_2O)_6]Cl_3$: $Fe^{3+}$ ($3d^5$) with WFL $\rightarrow t_{2g}^3 e_g^2$, $n = 5$.
Order of $n$: $0 < 1 < 4 < 5$. Statement II is true.
Both statements are true.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which one of the following complexes will have $\Delta_0=0$ and $\mu=5.96$ B.M.?
question: Which one of the following complexes will have $\Delta_0=0$ and $\mu=5.96$ B.M.?
solution:
(1) $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+} \quad \mathrm{Co}^{3+} \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 4 \mathrm{~s}^0$

$=[-0.4 \times 6+0.6 \times(0)] \Delta_0=-2.4 \Delta_0$
(2)
$\begin{aligned}
& {\left[\mathrm{Mn}(\mathrm{SCN})_6\right]^4} \\
& \mathrm{Mn}^{2+} \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 4 \mathrm{~s}^0
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mu=\sqrt{35} \text { B.M. }=5.96 \text { B.M. } \\
& \mathrm{CFSE}=(-0.4 \times 3+0.6 \times 2) \Delta_0
\end{aligned}$
So $\Delta_0=0$
(3)
$\begin{aligned}
& {\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{-4} \quad \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 4 \mathrm{~s}^0}
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{CFSE}=-2.4 \Delta_0$
(4) $\left[\mathrm{FeF}_6\right]^{4-}$
$\mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 4 \mathrm{~s}^0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mu=\sqrt{24} \text { B.M. }=4.89 \text { B.M. } \\
& \text { CFSE }=(-0.4 \times 4+0.6 \times 2) \Delta_0=-1.2 \Delta_0
\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following complexes will exhibit maximum attraction to an applied magnetic field?
question: Which of the following complexes will exhibit maximum attraction to an applied magnetic field?
solution:
Complex having maximum number of unpaired electrons will exhibit maximum attraction to applied magnetic field.

ZnH2O62+ →d10 system, t62g eg4, 0  unpaired electrons
CoH2O62+→d7 system, t52g eg2, 3 unpaired electrons
Co(en)33+→d6 system, t62g eg0, 0  unpaired electrons
 NiH2O62+→d8 system, t62g eg2, 2 unpaired electrons.

So Option D is correct.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Number of complexes from the following with even number of unpaired " \(\mathrm{d}\) " electrons is \(\qquad\) \(\left[\mathrm{V}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}\)
[Given atomic numbers : \(\mathrm{V}=23, \mathrm{Cr}=24, \mathrm{Fe}=26, \mathrm{Ni}=28 \mathrm{Cu}=29\)]
question:
Number of complexes from the following with even number of unpaired " \(\mathrm{d}\) " electrons is \(\qquad\) \(\left[\mathrm{V}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}\)
[Given atomic numbers : \(\mathrm{V}=23, \mathrm{Cr}=24, \mathrm{Fe}=26, \mathrm{Ni}=28 \mathrm{Cu}=29\)]
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & {\left[\mathrm{V}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+} \rightarrow \mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3} \\ & { }_{23} \mathrm{~V}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^3 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 \\ & \left.\mathrm{~V}^{+3}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^2, \mathrm{n}=2 \text { (even number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right) \\ & {\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & { }_{24} \mathrm{Cr}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 4 \mathrm{~s}^1 \\ & \left.\mathrm{Cr}^{+2}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4, \mathrm{n}=4 \text { (even number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & {\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 4 \mathrm{~s}^0 \\ & \mathrm{n}=5\left(\text { odd number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right) \\ & {\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & \mathrm{Ni}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^8 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 \\ & \left.\mathrm{Ni}^{+3}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^7, \mathrm{n}=3 \text { (odd number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right) \\ & {\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & \mathrm{Cu}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^9 4 \mathrm{~s}^0 \\ & \left.\mathrm{n}=1 \text { (odd number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right)\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
q:
Correct order of spin only magnetic moment of the following complex ions is:

(Given At. No. Fe: 26, Co: 27)
question:
Correct order of spin only magnetic moment of the following complex ions is:

(Given At. No. Fe: 26, Co: 27)
solution:
(1) FeF63-: Fe3+=3d5 (ΔO<P)

Number of unpaired e-=5

μ = n(n+2) BM

∴μ=35 BM

(2) CoF63-: Co3+=3d6ΔO<P

Number of unpaired e-=4

μ = n(n+2) BM

∴μ=24 BM

(3) CoC2O433-: Co3+=3d6ΔO>P

Number of unpaired e-=0

μ = n(n+2) BM

∴μ=0 BM

So, the correct order is FeF63->CoF63->CoC2O433-

subject: Chemistry
q: Spin only magnetic moment in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BM</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced><mi>CO</mi></mfenced><mn>4</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo></msup></math> is :
question: Spin only magnetic moment in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BM</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced><mi>CO</mi></mfenced><mn>4</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo></msup></math> is :
solution:
In Fe(CO)4C2O4+

Oxidation no. of Fe=+3 

26Fe+3=Ar183 d54 s0 

CO is a Strong field ligand, so paring occurs against the Hund's rule. 

Fe+3=t2g2,2,1 eg0,0 

Number of unpaired electron =1 

so magnetic moment μ=nn+2 B.M. =11+2=3=1.73 BM

q: The type of isomerism present in nitropentamine chromium (III) chloride is

subject: Chemistry
q: The correct IUPAC name of K2MnO4 is:
question: The correct IUPAC name of K2MnO4 is:
solution:
The structure of the IUPAC name is 

name of cationic part + Name of anionic part.

Metal present in the anionic complex, so 'ate', must be added at the end of the metal name. 

Oxidation state of Mn

Mn+4-2=-2                   =+6

The structure of anionic complex name is

ligand names + metal name(oxidation state)

K2MnO4

2+x–8=0

⇒x=+6

Oxidation state of Mn=+6

IUPAC Name is:

Potassium tetraoxidomanganate VI

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : Among $\left.\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)_{4}\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{en})_{3}\right)\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$ and $\left[\mathrm{Mn}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$, $\left[\mathrm{Mn}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$ has the maximum number of unpaired electrons.
Statement II : The number of pairs among $\left\{\left[\mathrm{NiCl}_{4}\right]^{2-},\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_{4}\right]\right\},\left\{\left[\mathrm{NiCl}_{4}\right]^{2-},\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}\right\}$ and $\left\{\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_{4}\right],\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}\right\}$ that contain only diamagnetic species is two.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below : Statement I is true but Statement II is false Both Statement I and Statement II are false
question:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : Among $\left.\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)_{4}\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{en})_{3}\right)\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$ and $\left[\mathrm{Mn}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$, $\left[\mathrm{Mn}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$ has the maximum number of unpaired electrons.
Statement II : The number of pairs among $\left\{\left[\mathrm{NiCl}_{4}\right]^{2-},\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_{4}\right]\right\},\left\{\left[\mathrm{NiCl}_{4}\right]^{2-},\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}\right\}$ and $\left\{\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_{4}\right],\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}\right\}$ that contain only diamagnetic species is two.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below : Statement I is true but Statement II is false Both Statement I and Statement II are false
options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I: Unpaired electrons in each complex:
$[Cu(NH_3)_4]^{2+}$: $Cu^{2+}$ is $d^9$ → 1 unpaired
$[Ni(en)_3]^{2+}$: $Ni^{2+}$ is $d^8$, octahedral → 2 unpaired
$[Ni(NH_3)_6]^{2+}$: $Ni^{2+}$ is $d^8$ → 2 unpaired
$[Mn(H_2O)_6]^{2+}$: $Mn^{2+}$ is $d^5$, weak field → high spin → 5 unpaired
$[Mn(H_2O)_6]^{2+}$ has maximum. Statement I is true.
Statement II: $[NiCl_4]^{2-}$: $Ni^{2+}$ ($d^8$), tetrahedral → paramagnetic
$[Ni(CO)_4]$: $Ni^0$ ($d^{10}$) → diamagnetic
$[Ni(CN)_4]^{2-}$: $Ni^{2+}$ ($d^8$), square planar → diamagnetic
Only pair $\{[Ni(CO)_4], [Ni(CN)_4]^{2-}\}$ has both diamagnetic. Count = 1, not 2. Statement II is false.

question:
The number of possible optical isomers for the complexes MA2B2 with sp3 and dsp2 hybridized metal atom, respectively, is:
Note: A and B are unidentate neutral and unidentate monoanionic ligands, respectively.
solution:
If the complex MA2B2 is  sp3 hybridised then the shape of this complex is tetrahedral this structure is optically inactive due to the presence of plane of symmetry



Optical isomers = 0 (b) If the complex MA 2 B 2  is   dsp 2 hybridised then the shape of this complex is square planar.



Both isomers are optically inactive due to the presence of plane of symmetry. Optical isomers =0

subject: Chemistry
q:
The mismatched combinations are

A. Chlorophyll–Co

B. Water hardness–EDTA

C. Photography-Ag(CN)2-

D. Wilkinson catalyst-Ph3P3RhCl

E. Chelating ligand–D-Penicillamine

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
question:
The mismatched combinations are

A. Chlorophyll–Co

B. Water hardness–EDTA

C. Photography-Ag(CN)2-

D. Wilkinson catalyst-Ph3P3RhCl

E. Chelating ligand–D-Penicillamine

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
solution:
The chlorophyll molecule has a central magnesium atom engulfed by a nitrogen-containing structure called a porphyrin ring; attached to the ring is a long carbon-hydrogen side chain.

Hardness of water is determined by titrating with a standard solution of ethylene diamine tetra acetic acid (EDTA) which is a complexing agent. Since EDTA is insoluble in water, the disodium salt of EDTA is taken for this experiment. EDTA can form four or six coordination bonds with a metal ion.

Silver bromide is used in photography as a component of an emulsion that helps develop a photographic image. Silver bromide is sensitive to light, and when suspended in gelatin, silver bromide's grains create a photographic emulsion.

Wilkinson's catalyst is the common name for chlorido­tris(triphenylphosphine)­rhodium(I), a coordination complex of rhodium with the formula Ph3P3RhCl

D-Penicillamine is chelating ligand.

subject: Chemistry
q: The total number of unpaired electrons present in the $d^3$, $d^4$ (low spin), $d^5$ (high spin), $d^6$ (high spin) and $d^7$ (low spin) octahedral complex systems is _______.
question: The total number of unpaired electrons present in the $d^3$, $d^4$ (low spin), $d^5$ (high spin), $d^6$ (high spin) and $d^7$ (low spin) octahedral complex systems is _______.
solution:
In an octahedral complex, the d-orbitals split into $t_{2g}$ and $e_g$ levels.

For $d^3$: Configuration is $t_{2g}^3 e_g^0$. Number of unpaired electrons = $3$.

For $d^4$ (low spin): Configuration is $t_{2g}^4 e_g^0$. Number of unpaired electrons = $2$.

For $d^5$ (high spin): Configuration is $t_{2g}^3 e_g^2$. Number of unpaired electrons = $5$.

For $d^6$ (high spin): Configuration is $t_{2g}^4 e_g^2$. Number of unpaired electrons = $4$.

For $d^7$ (low spin): Configuration is $t_{2g}^6 e_g^1$. Number of unpaired electrons = $1$.

Total number of unpaired electrons = $3 + 2 + 5 + 4 + 1 = 15$.

Answer: $15$

subject: Chemistry
q: In potassium ferrocyanide, there are ______ pairs of electrons in the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow></msub></math> set of orbitals.
question: In potassium ferrocyanide, there are ______ pairs of electrons in the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow></msub></math> set of orbitals.
solution: Potassium ferrocyanide, K4FeCN6, has a central iron ion that is coordinated with six cyanide ligands in an octahedral geometry. The oxidation state of the iron ion in potassium ferrocyanide is +2, hence has d2sp3 hybridised state. On applying C.F.T. it shows that all the 6 electrons of d subshell are present in the form of three pairs in t2g orbitals. Hence answer is 3.

subject: Chemistry
q: The correct increasing order of spin-only magnetic moment values of the complex ions $\left[\mathrm{MnBr}_{4}\right]^{2-}(\mathrm{A}),\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}(\mathrm{B}),\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}(\mathrm{C})$ and $\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}(\mathrm{D})$ is :
question: The correct increasing order of spin-only magnetic moment values of the complex ions $\left[\mathrm{MnBr}_{4}\right]^{2-}(\mathrm{A}),\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}(\mathrm{B}),\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}(\mathrm{C})$ and $\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}(\mathrm{D})$ is :
solution:
Calculate spin-only magnetic moment using $\mu_s = \sqrt{n(n+2)}$ where $n$ is the number of unpaired electrons.
For $[\text{MnBr}_4]^{2-}$ (A): Mn(II) in tetrahedral environment is high spin with 5 unpaired electrons. $\mu_s = \sqrt{5(7)} = \sqrt{35} \approx 5.92$ BM.
For $[\text{Cu}(\text{H}_2\text{O})_6]^{2+}$ (B): Cu(II) has 1 unpaired electron. $\mu_s = \sqrt{1(3)} = \sqrt{3} \approx 1.73$ BM.
For $[\text{Ni}(\text{CN})_4]^{2-}$ (C): Ni(II) in square planar geometry is low spin with 0 unpaired electrons. $\mu_s = 0$ BM.
For $[\text{Ni}(\text{H}_2\text{O})_6]^{2+}$ (D): Ni(II) in octahedral environment is high spin with 2 unpaired electrons. $\mu_s = \sqrt{2(4)} = \sqrt{8} \approx 2.83$ BM.
Increasing order: C (0) < B (1.73) < D (2.83) < A (5.92)

q:
Identify the homoleptic complex(es) that is/are low spin.
(A) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]^{2-}$
(B) $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{3-}$
(C) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{4-}$
(D) $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+}$
(E) $\quad\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}$

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Identify the homoleptic complex(es) that is/are low spin.
(A) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]^{2-}$
(B) $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{3-}$
(C) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{4-}$
(D) $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+}$
(E) $\quad\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}$

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
(A) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]^{-2} \rightarrow$ Heteroleptic, $\mathrm{Fe}^{+2}, 3 \mathrm{~d}^6$, $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}{ }^0, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$, Low spin ( 3 d series +SFL )
(B) $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{-3} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic, $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$, High spin, $\mathrm{Co}^{+3}, 3 \mathrm{~d}^6(3 \mathrm{~d}$ series + WFL $)$
(C) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{-4} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic
$\mathrm{Fe}^{+2}, 3 \mathrm{~d}^6, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3, \mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6 \mathrm{eg}^0$ Low spin
$(3 \mathrm{~d}$ series +SFL$)$
(D) $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{+3} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic, $\mathrm{Co}^{+3} 3 \mathrm{~d}^6, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$,
$\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6 \mathrm{eg}^0$, Low spin (3d series + SFL)
(E) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{+2} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic
$\mathrm{Cr}^{+2} 3 \mathrm{~d}^4, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$, High spin $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^3 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}{ }^1$
(3d series + WFL)

(b)NiH2O4NH322+  and

(c) NiH2O3NH332+ is:
options: (a) and (b) show geometrical and optical isomerism

solution:
NiH2O5NH32+ does not show any isomerism


NiH2O4NH322+  shows cis-trans (geometrical) isomerism

q: The spin-only magnetic moment value of an octahedral complex among <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CoCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>·</mo><mn>4</mn><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NiCl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>·</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PtCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>·</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>HCl</mi></math>, which upon reaction with excess of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>AgNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> moles of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AgCl</mi></math> is_____B.M. (Nearest Integer)
question: The spin-only magnetic moment value of an octahedral complex among <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CoCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>·</mo><mn>4</mn><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NiCl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>·</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PtCl</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>·</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>HCl</mi></math>, which upon reaction with excess of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>AgNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> moles of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AgCl</mi></math> is_____B.M. (Nearest Integer)
solution:
(I) H2PtCl6→ no ppt with AgNO3

Pt+4, CN=6

(II) NiH2O6Cl2 AgNO3excess 2 mole of AgCl↓,

Ni+2,CN=6,n=2, μm=nn+2 BM=8=2.82BM≃3

Aqua complexes, generally have CN=6

(III) CoCl2NH34Cl⟶AgNO3excess1 Mole of AgCl↓,

Co+3,CN=6

subject: Chemistry
q: In which one of the following metal carbonyls, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> forms a bridge between metal atoms?
question: In which one of the following metal carbonyls, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> forms a bridge between metal atoms?
solution: In the most common bridging mode, denoted μ2 or simply μ, the CO  ligand bridges a pair of metals. This bonding mode is observed in the commonly available metal carbonyls: Co2CO8, Fe2CO9, Fe3CO12and Co4CO12. In certain higher nuclearity clusters, CO bridges between three or even four metals.

q:
To inhibit the growth of tumours, identify the compounds used from the following:

(A) EDTA
(B) Coordination Compounds of Pt
(C) D-Penicillamine
(D) Cis - Platin

Choose the correct answer from the option given below:
question:
To inhibit the growth of tumours, identify the compounds used from the following:

(A) EDTA
(B) Coordination Compounds of Pt
(C) D-Penicillamine
(D) Cis - Platin

Choose the correct answer from the option given below:
solution: Cis - Platin PtNH32Cl2 is a chemotherapy medication used to treat a number of cancers. It is given by injection into a vein. Cisplatin binds to the N7 reactive center on purine residues and as such can cause deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) damage in cancer cells, blocking cell division and resulting in apoptotic cell death.

q: Considering that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Δ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>></mo><mi>P</mi></math>, the magnetic moment (in BM) of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Ru</mi><msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> would be
question: Considering that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Δ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>></mo><mi>P</mi></math>, the magnetic moment (in BM) of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Ru</mi><msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> would be
solution:
Ru2*=4d6=t2g2,2.2eg0,0 since Δ0>P
No. of unpaired electrons = zero
Magnetic Moment =0
